sections.paragraph: f. some domestic reptile, a digging animal. Jerome talpa, a mole; better perhaps mus major, rat, so called from digging. It appears that this name ought to be restored to the text in Isa 2:20, where now there is read separately, לַחְפֹּר פֵּרֹות i.e. “into the hole of the mice.” It would be much more suitable to the context to read לַחֲפַרְפֵּרֹות to the mice, or rats, or moles. Compare פֵּרָה.
